revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:ضريح توزان]][[commons:Category:Tōzan-jinja]][[en:Tōzan Shrine]][[fr:Tōzan-jinja]][[ja:陶山神社 (有田町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q251156]] Wikidata name: Tōzan Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture, Japan}}
{{Infobox religious building | name = Tōzan Shrine | image = Tozan Shrine01.jpg | caption = A porcelain ''torii'' archway | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|11|19.9|N|129|53|57.9|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:30000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }}
[[File:Komainu at Tozan Shrine in Arita, Saga Prefecture, made of porcelain.jpg|thumb|upright=1.2|right|A ''[[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]'' made of porcelain]] The {{nihongo|'''Tōzan''' or '''Sueyama Shrine'''|陶山神社}} is located in [[:en:Arita, Saga|Arita]], [[:en:Nishimatsuura District, Saga|Nishimatsuura District]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]. The shrine has a [[:en:Porcelain|Porcelain]] archway and other items of porcelain which, at other shrines, are usually made of stone. This [[Shinto]] shrine was and still is particularly revered by Arita's ceramists.

Retrieved 5 April 2024.</ref> In 1871, the shrine was renamed ''Sueyama-Jinja'' ({{lang|ja|陶山神社}}), literally "ceramic mountain shrine"; ''Tōzan'' is an [[Kanji#Readings|alternative reading]] of the ''kanji'' for ''Sueyama''.<ref name="MLIT"/> In 1917, a memorial to the semi-legendary potter [[:en:Yi Sam-pyeong|Yi Sam-pyeong]] (Kanagae Sambee) was built on a hill overlooking the town of Arita to commemorate the 300th anniversary of [[:en:Arita ware|Arita's porcelain]].<ref name="MLIT"/> [[File:Tozan Shrine 09 April 2011 01.jpg|thumb|upright=1.5|The view of Arita from the Yi Sam-pyeong Monument]]
The 1888 ''[[torii]]'' (a Shinto shrine archway), uniquely constructed of porcelain,<ref name="MLIT"/> was designated a [[:en:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan|Tangible Cultural Property]] on 28 April 2000.

The shrine has a [[:en:Porcelain|Porcelain]] archway and other items of porcelain which, at other shrines, are usually made of stone. This [[Shinto]] shrine was and still is particularly revered by Arita's ceramists.

Retrieved 5 April 2024.</ref> In 1871, the shrine was renamed ''Sueyama-Jinja'' ({{lang|ja|陶山神社}}), literally "ceramic mountain shrine"; ''Tōzan'' is an [[Kanji#Readings|alternative reading]] of the ''kanji'' for ''Sueyama''.<ref name="MLIT"/> In 1917, a memorial to the semi-legendary potter [[:en:Yi Sam-pyeong|Yi Sam-pyeong]] (Kanagae Sambee) was built on a hill overlooking the town of Arita to commemorate the 300th anniversary of [[:en:Arita ware|Arita's porcelain]].<ref name="MLIT"/> [[File:Tozan Shrine 09 April 2011 01.jpg|thumb|upright=1.5|The view of Arita from the Yi Sam-pyeong Monument]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tōzan Shrine | image = Tozan Shrine01.jpg | caption = A porcelain ''torii'' archway | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|11|19.9|N|129|53|57.9|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:30000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }}
[[File:Komainu at Tozan Shrine in Arita, Saga Prefecture, made of porcelain.jpg|thumb|upright=1.2|right|A ''[[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]'' made of porcelain]] The {{nihongo|'''Tōzan''' or '''Sueyama Shrine'''|陶山神社}} is located in [[:en:Arita, Saga|Arita]], [[:en:Nishimatsuura District, Saga|Nishimatsuura District]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]. The shrine has a [[:en:Porcelain|Porcelain]] archway and other items of porcelain which, at other shrines, are usually made of stone. This [[Shinto]] shrine was and still is particularly revered by Arita's ceramists.
The shrine was founded as Arita Sarayama Sōbyō [[Hachiman shrine|Hachimangū]] ({{lang|ja|有田皿山宗廟八幡宮}}) in 1658, and dedicated to [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and [[Nabeshima Naoshige]], the latter known for known for re-settling potters from Korea in the area.<ref name="MLIT">[https://www.mlit.go.jp/tagengo-db/common/001565488.pdf "Tozan Shrine."] Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism. Retrieved 5 April 2024.</ref> In 1871, the shrine was renamed ''Sueyama-Jinja'' ({{lang|ja|陶山神社}}), literally "ceramic mountain shrine"; ''Tōzan'' is an [[Kanji#Readings|alternative reading]] of the ''kanji'' for ''Sueyama''.<ref name="MLIT"/> In 1917, a memorial to the semi-legendary potter [[:en:Yi Sam-pyeong|Yi Sam-pyeong]] (Kanagae Sambee) was built on a hill overlooking the town of Arita to commemorate the 300th anniversary of [[:en:Arita ware|Arita's porcelain]].<ref name="MLIT"/> [[File:Tozan Shrine 09 April 2011 01.jpg|thumb|upright=1.5|The view of Arita from the Yi Sam-pyeong Monument]]
The 1888 ''[[torii]]'' (a Shinto shrine archway), uniquely constructed of porcelain,<ref name="MLIT"/> was designated a [[:en:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan|Tangible Cultural Property]] on 28 April 2000.

Retrieved 5 April 2024.</ref> In 1871, the shrine was renamed ''Sueyama-Jinja'' ({{lang|ja|陶山神社}}), literally "ceramic mountain shrine"; ''Tōzan'' is an [[Kanji#Readings|alternative reading]] of the ''kanji'' for ''Sueyama''.<ref name="MLIT"/> In 1917, a memorial to the semi-legendary potter [[:en:Yi Sam-pyeong|Yi Sam-pyeong]] (Kanagae Sambee) was built on a hill overlooking the town of Arita to commemorate the 300th anniversary of [[:en:Arita ware|Arita's porcelain]].<ref name="MLIT"/> [[File:Tozan Shrine 09 April 2011 01.jpg|thumb|upright=1.5|The view of Arita from the Yi Sam-pyeong Monument]]
The 1888 ''[[torii]]'' (a Shinto shrine archway), uniquely constructed of porcelain,<ref name="MLIT"/> was designated a [[:en:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan|Tangible Cultural Property]] on 28 April 2000.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:ضريح توزان]][[co:commons:Category:Tōzan-jinja]][[en:Tōzan Shrine]][[fr:Tōzan-jinja]][[ja:陶山神社 (有田町)]][[ko:스에야마 신사]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:ضريح توزان]][[co:commons:Category:Tōzan-jinja]][[en:Tōzan Shrine]][[fr:Tōzan-jinja]][[ja:陶山神社 (有田町)]][[ko:스에야마 신사]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:ضريح توزان]][[sn:commons:Category:Tōzan-jinja]][[en:Tōzan Shrine]][[fr:Tōzan-jinja]][[ja:陶山神社 (有田町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q251156]] Wikidata name: Tōzan Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture, Japan}}
{{Infobox religious building | name = Tōzan Shrine | image = Tozan Shrine01.jpg | caption = A porcelain ''torii'' archway | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|11|19.9|N|129|53|57.9|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:30000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }}
[[File:Komainu at Tozan Shrine in Arita, Saga Prefecture, made of porcelain.jpg|thumb|upright=1.2|right|A ''{{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu}}'' made of porcelain]] The {{nihongo|'''Tōzan''' or '''Sueyama Shrine'''|陶山神社}} is located in {{ill|Arita, Saga|en|Arita, Saga|WD=Q668427|lt=Arita}}, {{ill|Nishimatsuura District, Saga|en|Nishimatsuura District, Saga|WD=Q1132194|lt=Nishimatsuura District}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}}. The shrine has a {{ill|Porcelain|en|Porcelain|WD=Q130693|lt=Porcelain}} archway and other items of porcelain which, at other shrines, are usually made of stone. This [[Shinto]] shrine was and still is particularly revered by Arita's ceramists.
The shrine was founded as Arita Sarayama Sōbyō [[Hachiman shrine|Hachimangū]] ({{lang|ja|有田皿山宗廟八幡宮}}) in 1658, and dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and [[Nabeshima Naoshige]], the latter known for known for re-settling potters from Korea in the area.<ref name="MLIT">[https://www.mlit.go.jp/tagengo-db/common/001565488.pdf "Tozan Shrine."] Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism. Retrieved 5 April 2024.</ref> In 1871, the shrine was renamed ''Sueyama-Jinja'' ({{lang|ja|陶山神社}}), literally "ceramic mountain shrine"; ''Tōzan'' is an [[Kanji#Readings|alternative reading]] of the ''kanji'' for ''Sueyama''.<ref name="MLIT"/> In 1917, a memorial to the semi-legendary potter {{ill|Yi Sam-pyeong|en|Yi Sam-pyeong|WD=Q186902|lt=Yi Sam-pyeong}} (Kanagae Sambee) was built on a hill overlooking the town of Arita to commemorate the 300th anniversary of {{ill|Arita ware|en|Arita ware|WD=Q26273242|lt=Arita's porcelain}}.<ref name="MLIT"/> [[File:Tozan Shrine 09 April 2011 01.jpg|thumb|upright=1.5|The view of Arita from the Yi Sam-pyeong Monument]]
The 1888 ''[[torii]]'' (a Shinto shrine archway), uniquely constructed of porcelain,<ref name="MLIT"/> was designated a [[:en:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan|Tangible Cultural Property]] on 28 April 2000.
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://web.archive.org/web/20080211045354/http://www.arita-toso.com/ Sueyama Shrine Website] {{in lang|ja}}
{{commons Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category|Tōzan-jinja|Tōzan Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tōzan Shrine | image = Tozan Shrine01.jpg | caption = A porcelain ''torii'' archway | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|11|19.9|N|129|53|57.9|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:30000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }}
[[File:Komainu at Tozan Shrine in Arita, Saga Prefecture, made of porcelain.jpg|thumb|upright=1.2|right|A ''{{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu}}'' made of porcelain]] The {{nihongo|'''Tōzan''' or '''Sueyama Shrine'''|陶山神社}} is located in {{ill|Arita, Saga|en|Arita, Saga|WD=Q668427|lt=Arita}}, {{ill|Nishimatsuura District, Saga|en|Nishimatsuura District, Saga|WD=Q1132194|lt=Nishimatsuura District}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}}. The shrine has a {{ill|Porcelain|en|Porcelain|WD=Q130693|lt=Porcelain}} archway and other items of porcelain which, at other shrines, are usually made of stone. This [[Shinto]] shrine was and still is particularly revered by Arita's ceramists.
The shrine was founded as Arita Sarayama Sōbyō [[Hachiman shrine|Hachimangū]] ({{lang|ja|有田皿山宗廟八幡宮}}) in 1658, and dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and [[Nabeshima Naoshige]], the latter known for known for re-settling potters from Korea in the area.<ref name="MLIT">[https://www.mlit.go.jp/tagengo-db/common/001565488.pdf "Tozan Shrine."] Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism. Retrieved 5 April 2024.</ref> In 1871, the shrine was renamed ''Sueyama-Jinja'' ({{lang|ja|陶山神社}}), literally "ceramic mountain shrine"; ''Tōzan'' is an [[Kanji#Readings|alternative reading]] of the ''kanji'' for ''Sueyama''.<ref name="MLIT"/> In 1917, a memorial to the semi-legendary potter {{ill|Yi Sam-pyeong|en|Yi Sam-pyeong|WD=Q186902|lt=Yi Sam-pyeong}} (Kanagae Sambee) was built on a hill overlooking the town of Arita to commemorate the 300th anniversary of {{ill|Arita ware|en|Arita ware|WD=Q26273242|lt=Arita's porcelain}}.<ref name="MLIT"/> [[File:Tozan Shrine 09 April 2011 01.jpg|thumb|upright=1.5|The view of Arita from the Yi Sam-pyeong Monument]]
The 1888 ''[[torii]]'' (a Shinto shrine archway), uniquely constructed of porcelain,<ref name="MLIT"/> was designated a [[:en:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan|Tangible Cultural Property]] on 28 April 2000.

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tōzan Shrine | image = Tozan Shrine01.jpg | caption = A porcelain ''torii'' archway | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|11|19.9|N|129|53|57.9|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:30000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }}
[[File:Komainu at Tozan Shrine in Arita, Saga Prefecture, made of porcelain.jpg|thumb|upright=1.2|right|A ''{{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu|1=Komainu}}'' made of porcelain]] The {{nihongo|'''Tōzan''' or '''Sueyama Shrine'''|陶山神社}} is located in {{ill|Arita, Saga|en|Arita, Saga|WD=Q668427|lt=Arita|1=Arita, Saga}}, {{ill|Nishimatsuura District, Saga|en|Nishimatsuura District, Saga|WD=Q1132194|lt=Nishimatsuura District|1=Nishimatsuura District, Saga}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}}. The shrine has a {{ill|Porcelain|en|Porcelain|WD=Q130693|lt=Porcelain|1=Porcelain}} archway and other items of porcelain which, at other shrines, are usually made of stone. This [[Shinto]] shrine was and still is particularly revered by Arita's ceramists.
The shrine was founded as Arita Sarayama Sōbyō [[Hachiman shrine|Hachimangū]] ({{lang|ja|有田皿山宗廟八幡宮}}) in 1658, and dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and [[Nabeshima Naoshige]], the latter known for known for re-settling potters from Korea in the area.<ref name="MLIT">[https://www.mlit.go.jp/tagengo-db/common/001565488.pdf "Tozan Shrine."] Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism. Retrieved 5 April 2024.</ref> In 1871, the shrine was renamed ''Sueyama-Jinja'' ({{lang|ja|陶山神社}}), literally "ceramic mountain shrine"; ''Tōzan'' is an [[Kanji#Readings|alternative reading]] of the ''kanji'' for ''Sueyama''.<ref name="MLIT"/> In 1917, a memorial to the semi-legendary potter {{ill|Yi Sam-pyeong|en|Yi Sam-pyeong|WD=Q186902|lt=Yi Sam-pyeong|1=Yi Sam-pyeong}} (Kanagae Sambee) was built on a hill overlooking the town of Arita to commemorate the 300th anniversary of {{ill|Arita ware|en|Arita ware|WD=Q26273242|lt=Arita's porcelain|1=Arita ware}}.<ref name="MLIT"/> [[File:Tozan Shrine 09 April 2011 01.jpg|thumb|upright=1.5|The view of Arita from the Yi Sam-pyeong Monument]]
The 1888 ''[[torii]]'' (a Shinto shrine archway), uniquely constructed of porcelain,<ref name="MLIT"/> was designated a [[:en:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan|Tangible Cultural Property]] on 28 April 2000.
